methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Shine Technologies, a Series E climate and energy company founded by Greg Piefer, has raised over $1.0B to date. The valuation analysis draws on a competitive set of nine same-sector peers, including one public company (Lnza) and eight private firms such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow comparables—zero companies matched on both stage and valuation tier, with only two adjacent-stage peers identified, falling short of the three-datapoint threshold needed for reliable triangulation.

Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using the $1.0B total capital raised as a proxy baseline, a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement yields an implied value of $1.4B. This represents the single quantitative anchor available from the data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $1.4B. Key uncertainties stem from the absence of sufficiently comparable peers at Shine's stage and scale; a broader peer cohort or additional same-stage transactions in frontier climate tech would materially improve confidence. The current estimate should be treated as illustrative pending access to tighter comparables or transaction-based calibration.
